label: 30-year fixed mortgage rate (California average)
notes: Rates move daily. National avg ~6.47–6.51%. Dipped below 6% in Feb 2026, rose on geopolitical tension. FHA ~6.10%, VA ~5.84%. Always get live quotes from 3+ lenders.

label: 2026 conforming (conventional) loan limit, Riverside County, 1-unit
notes: National baseline, up $26,250 from 2025's $806,500. Riverside is NOT a high-cost county, so it uses the baseline (not the $1,249,125 ceiling). VA follows this same limit.

label: 2026 FHA loan limit, Riverside County, 1-unit
notes: Single aggregated source — verify at HUD before relying on it. A median Murrieta home (~$690k) sits right at this limit, so FHA is viable but tight there.

label: 2026 USDA guaranteed loan limit, Riverside County, 1-unit
notes: USDA 0%-down loans require the property be in an eligible rural area — parts of outer Riverside County (near Lake Elsinore/Menifee fringes) may qualify. Check the USDA eligibility map for a specific address.

label: California Dream For All — program structure
value: Up to 20% of purchase price as a shared-appreciation loan; deferred, no monthly payment, no interest. Repay original amount + same % of appreciation on sale/refi. Lottery-based, limited funding.
notes: Launched 2023 ($300M gone in 11 days); lottery format since 2024. It is NOT a monthly bill — it comes out of your sale proceeds. This is separate from a Section 8 rental voucher.

label: California Dream For All — income limit, Riverside/Inland Empire
notes: Set per county + household size on HUD AMI; the source explicitly calls these approximate. Counts income of ALL adults over 18 in the household, not just borrowers. MUST confirm the exact figure at calhfa.ca.gov.

label: California home insurance market status
value: In active reform after Jan 2025 LA wildfires. Major insurers pulled back; FAIR Plan (last resort) covers 555k+ homes. Sustainable Insurance Strategy now requires cat-model insurers to write >=85% market share in wildfire areas.
source: CA Dept of Insurance / Gov. Newsom / Insurance Journal
notes: SW Riverside has wildfire exposure. NO INSURANCE = NO LOAN — get an insurance quote before committing to a specific home. Reforms may raise rates 30-40% but restore availability. Note: a 2025 appellate ruling (Gharibian v. Wawanesa) denied wildfire smoke-damage coverage.

label: Median/average home price, Murrieta CA
notes: Down ~1.3% YoY. Market cooling toward buyers: rising days-on-market, ~2.9 months inventory, ~41% of sales included buyer concessions averaging ~$21,823.

label: Median/average home price, Temecula CA
notes: Down ~1.4% YoY, ~$358/sqft. Same cooling trend as Murrieta — buyer concessions common.

label: California property tax (Prop 13)
value: ~1.1–1.25% of purchase price per year (1% base + local voter-approved assessments), capped at ~2%/yr growth. Basis = your purchase price.
notes: Mechanics are stable (bake-worthy). BUT Mello-Roos special taxes (very common in SW Riverside new developments) add $100-400+/mo ON TOP and are per-development — check the specific property's tax bill. Expect a one-time SUPPLEMENTAL tax bill after closing from reassessment.
